title: Ratify consent record schema + speaker release + withdrawal terms
context: Voice recordings are personal data. Before any audio is ingested, the project needs a machine-readable ConsentRecord schema that captures every dimension required for legal clarity and community trust: the license granted (CC-BY-4.0 or CC0-1.0), explicit public-reuse and voice-recording release, a withdrawal process with honest language about third-party copies, a CARE/community-stewardship flag for Indigenous/minority-language entries, the verification method, and a date. Speakers may be minors or belong to vulnerable groups, requiring an extra-review clause. The schema must be Ajv-valid so the CI consent-completeness gate can enforce it mechanically. No data ingestion (M1+) may begin until this is ratified. Depends on place-name-pronunciation-schema-001.
objective: Produce a ratified, Ajv-valid ConsentRecord JSON Schema and a matching human-readable speaker release template, covering: license grant, explicit voice-recording release for public reuse, withdrawal terms (with honest third-party-copy caveat), CARE/community-stewardship flag and terms, date, and verification method; plus a guardian-consent clause for minors/vulnerable speakers.
acceptanceCriteria: ConsentRecord JSON Schema is Ajv-valid (draft-07) and covers: id, scope, licenseGranted (enum: CC-BY-4.0 | CC0-1.0), explicitVoiceRelease (boolean), withdrawalTerms (string), careFlag (boolean), careTerms (string, required when careFlag=true), date, verificationMethod.
Withdrawal terms text states plainly that future corpus exports will drop withdrawn recordings but that third-party copies already distributed cannot be recalled.
A human-readable speaker release template (Markdown) derived from the schema is provided and reviewed by the privacy/consent reviewer.
Guardian-consent clause for minors/vulnerable speakers is defined (extra review required; default is to avoid without a partner protocol).
Schema integrates with the Recording record's mandatory consentRef slot defined in place-name-pronunciation-schema-001.
Privacy/consent reviewer signs off the ratified schema and template before the task is marked done.
output: Ajv-valid ConsentRecord JSON Schema file plus a human-readable speaker release template (Markdown), committed via PR with privacy/consent reviewer sign-off.
